taxonomy: Squilla serotina is classified within the phylum Arthropoda, class Malacostraca, order Stomatopoda, and family Squillidae. It belongs to the genus Squilla and is often referred to as the "mole crab" due to its burrowing habits. The specific name serotina likely indicates that it is a late-flowering or late-hatching species. This classification places Squilla serotina within a group of crustaceans known as stomatopods, which are characterized by their distinctive mouthparts and powerful claws. Squilla serotina is primarily found in tropical and subtropical marine environments around the world.

preparations: Squilla serotina is also known as Sea Squill or Sea Squill bulb, and it is used to create herbal preparations.
